failure_modes.why: Blue built only its two starting settlements and a string of roads, never adding a single new settlement or city after t2.
Blue stayed entirely on settlements and never upgraded to cities, forfeiting the doubled production and VP that Red used to win.
Blue spent dozens of maritime trades churning resources into bricks/ore/wheat without converting them into any new VP-producing build.

review_summary: Blue froze at 2 settlements and wasted its turns on roads and 3:1 trades while Red relentlessly expanded and upgraded to cities to reach 10.

0.summary: Node 25 is a defensible opening with a strong ORE port and zero oracle regret, though the resource base is somewhat narrow.

28.summary: Buying a development card with an unbuildable hand is a defensible, value-equivalent tempo play that builds toward knights or VP.

83.summary: A reasonable early road extension to relieve a boxed-in position, with zero oracle regret.

144.summary: A defensible 3:1 sheep-to-wheat trade dumping a surplus resource, though buying a dev card was an equally reasonable alternative given the boxed-in board state.

206.summary: Building the city on node 28 is a strong, zero-regret tempo move advancing RED's VP lead.

251.summary: A defensible bank trade converting surplus sheep into needed wheat, though no immediate build results.

296.summary: A defensible 4:1 bank conversion of surplus brick into wheat to advance toward a city while leading.

349.summary: A defensible bank trade for brick that modestly helps, though no immediate build results and the oracle slightly preferred wheat.

474.summary: Optimal robber placement on BLUE that blocks production and steals, matching the oracle exactly.

failed.claude: Trading 2 wheat for a 4th brick when already holding 3 brick is poor value; ore or sheep would diversify toward a settlement/dev build far better.
failed.openai: Brick is more useful for building roads and settlements than excess wheat.

failed.claude: Now holds 4 brick but no wood/ore, so this doesn't complete any build—getting ore would have advanced toward a settlement or dev card.
failed.openai: The trade for brick supports building roads or settlements, advancing toward victory.

419.summary: A wasteful trade converting useful wheat into a fourth brick that advances nothing—ore or simply ending the turn was clearly better.

523.summary: A reasonable bank trade toward WHEAT matching the oracle's top choice, defensible despite BLUE's losing position.

568.summary: Building the VP-advancing settlement on node 0 is an optimal, zero-regret move that pushes RED toward victory.

661.summary: A clean bank trade converting surplus brick into needed wheat to push toward the winning VP.

failed.claude: RED is assembling resources to push toward a winning build, so converting excess brick to needed wood has some constructive purpose though it doesn't itself complete a city.
failed.openai: The trade does not advance towards building a city, which is needed to win; trading for ore would have been better.

617.summary: A defensible bank trade converting surplus brick into a needed resource while leading, even if 4:1 ratios are inherently inefficient.

712.summary: BLUE correctly placed the robber to disrupt and steal from the near-winning opponent, an essentially optimal defensive play.
